visual_checks: Check if the login or authentication process requires memorizing or transcribing information
Look for CAPTCHAs that require cognitive function tests such as puzzles, math problems, or pattern recognition
Check if password entry supports paste functionality and password managers
Look for authentication steps that require recognizing or manipulating objects (e.g., image-based challenges)
Verify that authentication does not require recalling specific information like a username without autofill support
Check for any cognitive function test at any step of the authentication process
pass_conditions: Authentication does not require any cognitive function test at any step
Password fields support paste and autofill from password managers
Alternative authentication methods are available that do not require cognitive function tests (e.g., passkeys, email magic links, biometric authentication)
No CAPTCHAs, puzzles, or cognitive challenges are used at any point in authentication
Users can authenticate using assistive technology without performing memory, transcription, or recognition tasks
fail_conditions: A CAPTCHA or puzzle must be solved to authenticate, even if an alternative CAPTCHA type is offered
Users must transcribe or retype a code from one device or context to another
Password fields block paste functionality, preventing password manager use
Authentication requires recognizing previously chosen images or patterns (cognitive recognition test)
Any step of the authentication process requires a cognitive function test with no non-cognitive alternative
na_conditions: Page has no authentication, login, or account verification process
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
The full authentication flow could not be tested from the current page
common_mistakes: Image-based CAPTCHAs that require identifying objects (e.g., 'select all traffic lights') with no non-cognitive alternative
Blocking paste in password or verification code fields, defeating password managers
Requiring users to retype a code from an email or SMS without allowing paste
Security questions that require recalling personal information from memory
Two-factor authentication that requires manually transcribing a code rather than supporting autofill or push-based approval

examples.pass: A login page supports username and password autofill from password managers, offers a passkey or magic link option, and has no CAPTCHA or cognitive challenge at any step of the authentication process
examples.fail: A login page requires solving a visual CAPTCHA puzzle (drag a piece to complete an image) and the password field has autocomplete='off' and blocks paste — users must both transcribe their password from memory and solve a cognitive test
examples.partial: The primary login supports password manager autofill and has no CAPTCHA, but the account recovery flow requires solving an image recognition CAPTCHA with no non-cognitive alternative

plain_meaning: Same rule as SC 3.3.8 but stricter: object recognition and personal content are NO LONGER valid exceptions. Authentication must not require any cognitive function test unless an alternative method or an assistive mechanism (like password-manager support) is available.
scope_applies_to: All the same authentication flows covered by SC 3.3.8
Any cognitive challenge in the authentication path
scope_does_not_apply_to: Magic-link email flows without additional cognitive steps
Flows where an alternative non-cognitive path is offered
Situations covered entirely by password-manager support
borderline_calibration: This is Level AAA and stricter than 3.3.8.
Object-recognition CAPTCHAs DO NOT satisfy this SC (unlike 3.3.8).
Personal content recognition also does not satisfy.
Only Alternative method or Mechanism (password-manager support etc.) satisfies.
Prefer Not Applicable outside AAA reviews.
